methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $7M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Navier, a seed-stage transportation company, has raised $7.2M to date. The broader transportation sector includes five private peers (Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, REGENT Craft) and no public comparables, limiting peer-based valuation approaches.

Given data constraints, stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable lens. Using total capital raised ($7M) as a proxy for seed-stage valuation and applying a standard 2.5x step-up multiplier to the next funding stage yields an implied valuation of $18M. This method reflects typical risk-adjusted capital efficiency at the seed-to-Series A transition in transportation, where capital requirements and technological validation typically drive material step-ups.

The peer-median approach is inapplicable: while five transportation companies exist in the comp universe, none meet minimum thresholds for same-stage or similar-tier peer grouping at seed stage. Drawing on all-sector medians would introduce unjustified noise. The analysis therefore supports a valuation range of $15M to $21M, centered at $18M. Key uncertainties include product development milestones, path to next institutional funding, and whether Navier's specific technology verticalization (within transportation) commands premium or discount to stage norms. The single-method reliance indicates valuation confidence is moderate and heavily contingent on execution between seed and Series A.
